title: podman save has problem with multi-image docker archive
body:
/kind bug

Podman save make broken docker archive with only one image id from many.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
```
bash-4.2# podman version
Version:      2.2.0
API Version:  2.1.0
Go Version:   go1.15.2
Built:        Tue Dec  1 02:59:37 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64

bash-4.2# podman pull docker.io/library/busybox:1.32.0
bash-4.2# podman pull docker.io/library/busybox:1.31.0

bash-4.2# podman images
REPOSITORY                 TAG     IMAGE ID      CREATED        SIZE
docker.io/library/busybox  1.32.0  219ee5171f80  3 days ago     1.45 MB
docker.io/library/busybox  1.31.0  19485c79a9bb  15 months ago  1.44 MB

bash-4.2# podman save docker.io/library/busybox:1.32.0 docker.io/library/busybox:1.31.0 > /mnt/test4.tar
Copying blob de168d3b8ec4 done
Copying config 219ee5171f done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures

bash-4.2# podman rmi docker.io/library/busybox:1.32.0 docker.io/library/busybox:1.31.0
Untagged: docker.io/library/busybox:1.32.0
Untagged: docker.io/library/busybox:1.31.0
Deleted: 219ee5171f8006d1462fa76c12b9b01ab672dbc8b283f186841bf2c3ca8e3c93

bash-4.2# podman images
REPOSITORY  TAG     IMAGE ID  CREATED  SIZE

bash-4.2# cat /mnt/test4.tar | podman load
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob de168d3b8ec4 done
Copying config 219ee5171f done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob de168d3b8ec4 [--------------------------------------] 0.0b / 0.0b
Copying config 219ee5171f done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
Loaded image(s): docker.io/library/busybox:1.32.0, docker.io/library/busybox:1.31.0

bash-4.2# podman images
REPOSITORY                 TAG     IMAGE ID      CREATED     SIZE
docker.io/library/busybox  1.31.0  219ee5171f80  3 days ago  1.45 MB
docker.io/library/busybox  1.32.0  219ee5171f80  3 days ago  1.45 MB
```

comments.body: @QiWang19 @vrothberg What is the latest on this?
Ok, `-m` can be dropped since both docker, oci archive support multi-image.

> Ok, `-m` can be dropped since both docker, oci archive support multi-image.

Let's not remove it entirely (to not break existing deployments) but hide it in the CLI and make it a nop.
